title: NMR: Terminal Methyl Multiplicity
bodyText: A terminal $CH_3$ group next to a $CH_2$ group follows the $n+1$ rule. Type its expected multiplicity as one word.

figures.description: Mock placeholder: normally this would show the expanded methyl signal with three lines of roughly equal spacing.

correctFeedback: Correct: the adjacent CH2 has two protons, so n+1 gives three lines.
incorrectFeedback: Not quite. A CH3 next to one CH2 has two neighboring protons, so n+1 gives three lines.
